current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.

recent_results.description: Run a `factorized_topk` proxy stabilization pass at `EXPANSION_FACTOR=32`, `K=128`, `LR=1.6e-3`, and `AUXK_ALPHA=0.03125` to test whether stronger aux-k regularization improves the best current factorized setup.

Add a `residual_topk` prototype in `sparsify/` and run a K=128 proxy comparison under the current best signum regime to test whether two-stage sparse residual coding can beat flat top-k-style encoders.
Register and implement the `residual_topk` architecture in `sparsify/`, then rerun the same K=128 proxy prototype under the current best signum settings.

recent_round_summaries.hypothesis: The EF=32 `factorized_topk` variant improved with more capacity, but lowering LR regressed sharply, so the family now looks more sensitive to support-shaping regularization than to further optimizer slowdown. The cleanest remaining single-variable stabilization check is to raise `AUXK_ALPHA` on the best factorized recipe (EF=32, LR=1.6e-3) and test whether stronger dead-feature recovery improves utilization enough to recover proxy FVU.

Five rounds of factorized-topk stabilization failed to approach the gated frontier, so that direction is now effectively exhausted rather than merely under-tuned. The next mandatory informative move is a fundamentally different architecture family: a `residual_topk` SAE that spends the same total K budget across two sequential sparse stages, letting a second sparse pass model the first-pass residual instead of forcing one flat support set to capture everything at once.

`residual_topk` did not fail on quality or runtime grounds; it failed because the execution layer still does not recognize the architecture name. The smallest informative next move is a narrow code-fix round that registers and wires `residual_topk` into the existing config/class dispatch and forward path so the prototype can finally receive one real K=128 proxy measurement under the current strongest regime.

residual_topk.design_hypothesis: `residual_topk` did not fail on quality or runtime grounds; it failed because the execution layer still does not recognize the architecture name. The smallest informative next move is a narrow code-fix round that registers and wires `residual_topk` into the existing config/class dispatch and forward path so the prototype can finally receive one real K=128 proxy measurement under the current strongest regime.
next_steps: If `residual_topk` runs and shows promising proxy FVU, keep the family alive and stabilize its internal K split next.
If it runs cleanly but is clearly worse than gated and not better than factorized-topk, archive it quickly and move to another architecture family.
If `residual_topk` still fails after registration is fixed, treat the remaining issue as a deeper implementation incompatibility and shift effort elsewhere.

recent_performance_findings: round 41: The recent `first_step_timeout` crashes are not specific to `group_topk`: the gated AUXK=0 recovery run also advanced through the first few microbatches and then stalled at the first optimizer step. That points to a shared training-path bottleneck rather than an architecture verdict. Replacing CUDA's per-step fired-latent `cat`+advanced-index update with an incremental boolean fired mask in `sparsify/trainer.py` should remove that first-step spike and let the unresolved `group_topk` K=128 proxy run complete under the current strongest regime. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=None, k=None, health=crash, termination=sanity_failed)

round 46: The obvious gated one-parameter sweep is now exhausted after clean regressions on both lower LR and lower expansion, so the next least-bad informative move is to unblock the incubating `factorized_topk` family rather than introduce another fresh direction. Its only evidence so far is a `sanity_failed` prototype, which should be treated as an architecture-compatibility issue, not a quality result. A narrow code fix inside `sparsify/` to make `factorized_topk` forward/backward compatible with the runtime should recover one valid proxy measurement at K=128 under the existing strongest regime. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=None, k=None, health=crash, termination=sanity_failed)

round 47: The straightforward gated hyperparameter sweep has now produced only clean regressions, so the next informative move is to recover the blocked incubating family rather than spend another round on weak local tuning. `factorized_topk` still has no usable quality signal because its only attempt failed at sanity. A narrow architecture-compatibility fix inside `sparsify/` should let the same K=128 proxy prototype run and finally reveal whether extra encoder mixing can improve over plain top-k. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=, k=None, health=normal, termination=completed)

round 49: `factorized_topk` has now failed for a concrete architecture-registration reason rather than an ambiguous quality or runtime signal: the execution layer rejects the architecture name in `SparseCoderConfig` before training begins. The next coherent move is a narrow code-fix round that wires `factorized_topk` into the existing config/class registry and forward path so the prototype can finally receive one real K=128 proxy measurement under the strongest current regime. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=, k=None, health=crash, termination=completed)

Its only evidence so far is a `sanity_failed` prototype, which should be treated as an architecture-compatibility issue, not a quality result. A narrow code fix inside `sparsify/` to make `factorized_topk` forward/backward compatible with the runtime should recover one valid proxy measurement at K=128 under the existing strongest regime.

recent_training_failures.hypothesis: Plain gated/local parameter sweeps have revalidated the low-K frontier but are not closing the quality gap, and the session has gone eight rounds without advancing a new family. A minimal `factorized_topk` prototype that inserts a low-rank ReLU mixing stage before hard top-k selection could improve support quality at K=128 while staying cheaper than a full dense two-branch encoder, so the next round should finally give this family a real proxy measurement under the current strongest signum regime.

Five rounds of factorized-topk stabilization failed to approach the gated frontier, so that direction is now effectively exhausted rather than merely under-tuned. The next mandatory informative move is a fundamentally different architecture family: a `residual_topk` SAE that spends the same total K budget across two sequential sparse stages, letting a second sparse pass model the first-pass residual instead of forcing one flat support set to capture everything at once.
